remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: GAD: spontaneous remission in ~25-30% at 1 year without treatment; social anxiety: 36% remission at 12 years untreated; panic disorder: 50-65% spontaneous partial remission but relapse common; 40-60% remain symptomatic at 5 years
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: CBT (gold standard): 60-80% treatment response, 40-60% full remission at 12-24 weeks; combination CBT + SSRI superior to either alone in some disorders; exposure therapy for specific phobias: 90%+ response; SSRI/SNRI alone: 50-60% response
remission.gap_size: Large — CBT is consistently superior to medication for long-term outcomes but is massively underused relative to pharmacotherapy
remission.barrier_detail: SSRIs/SNRIs are first-line by default in most primary care settings; CBT is guideline-recommended but wait times of 6-18 months are common; digital CBT (iCBT) is evidence-based but rarely prescribed; benzodiazepines continue to be prescribed long-term despite dependence risk
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — FDA approved brexanolone for postpartum anxiety/depression; 2022 buspirone repositioning; first-line SSRIs/SNRIs essentially unchanged since early 1990s; duloxetine for GAD added 2004
remission.outdated_treatments: Benzodiazepine long-term prescribing for chronic anxiety (dependence, cognitive impairment, falls in elderly; guidelines recommend short-term only; yet 30-40% of long-term BZD users have anxiety disorders with chronic prescriptions); propranolol monotherapy for social anxiety; barbiturates (rare but still encountered in elderly patients on legacy prescriptions)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Mindfulness-based interventions (MBSR equivalent to CBT in meta-analysis); exercise (particularly aerobic — RCT evidence for GAD and panic disorder); magnesium glycinate supplementation (anxiolytic effects in mild-moderate anxiety; RCT data); kava kava (Cochrane review: superior to placebo, safety concerns with hepatotoxicity); breathing-based techniques (cyclic sighing — Nature Medicine 2023)
remission.frontier_signal: MDMA-assisted therapy for PTSD/social anxiety (FDA PDUFA date 2024 — initial rejection, resubmission); psilocybin for treatment-resistant anxiety (Phase 2 at Imperial College, Johns Hopkins); ketamine nasal spray for acute anxiety (Phase 2); CGRP antagonists for comorbid migraine-anxiety
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
